A(rt): More Possibilities in STEAM: A(rt): More Possibilities in STEAM is a club dedicated to exploring and recognizing the 'A' in STEM as a vital part of this rising field. Through a guest speaker, discussion, and activity-oriented club, our goal is to meet people who are experts in their STEAM-related careers and make lifelong connections. Some of the topics we discuss are the importance of STEAM in one's academic and professional lives, AI's rise in fashion, UI/UX design, architecture and structural engineering, and exploring non-profits and businesses related to STEAM. As guest speakers, we will have a UI/UX designer, a structural engineer, and business and non-profit founders come in to share their message and stories. Anyone and everyone are welcome!
ABC: Academy Book Club: Students at Exeter are faced with a heavy workload and often don't have time to read personal books. We would love to create a relaxing, low key environment for that through ABC. Our goal is to provide a space for Exonians to free read each week, stress- and phone-free to further foster their love for literature. We would like to explore different literary tastes and venture into different genres that we wouldn't typically have exposure to in a normal classroom environment. If students wish, they can also discuss books with other members of the club to enhance their harkness skills. We would love to have authors come and speak, have a small book exchange, and open the space to English teachers as well.
Academy Band of Lego Builders: This is a club for all individuals who are inspired to create and have fun with Lego. Participants in this club will learn how to use simple software to design their own Lego creations. Through a series of individual and group activities, students will create their own Lego designs, as well as collaborate on designing and building a larger Lego set, such as a replica of the Phillips Exeter Library. Everyone from Lego novices to master builders have a place in this club!
Academy Belly Dancing Society: ABS is a dance club where we belly dance to modern songs as well as traditional belly dancing beats. Contrary to the name you definitely do not need abs (nor prior experience) to try out!! If you are interested in trying out, please email one of the co-heads so that we can send more information.
Activism in Art: Activism in Art Club is about using creativity to speak up and spark change. During our meetings, we'll have time to work independently on art projects that reflect what matters to us personally, as well as collaborate on group pieces that support larger causes. We're excited to work with clubs like Unsilenced, the Lamont Gallery, and other activism-based groups on campus to make our work visible and meaningful, whether that's through posters, pop-up galleries, fundraising with our art, or partnering with local nonprofits to bring our message beyond PEA. Some weeks we might focus on creating art for awareness campaigns around issues like climate justice, racial equity, LGBTQ+ rights, gender equality, or mental health. Other times, we'll spend time learning about artists who have used their work for activism, and how we can do the same in our own way.
Admissions Tour Guides: The Admissions Tour Guides are often prospective students' first connection with Phillips Exeter Academy. After current students complete training, they are able to provide tours to families visiting campus, and serve as liaisons for our community. Students are able to join this club and conduct as many tours during their free time as they would like! Snacks, hot cocoa, apple cider & donuts are often provided as a "thank you!"
African Student Association: ASA (African students association) invites those who identify as African, have ties to their African roots and also seek to explore and strengthen their ancestral ties to Africa to join our club. We aim to create an open space for dialogue to grow and strengthen the bond between the African community on campus. Celebrating the richness of culture, language, food, etc. of the different African countries represented on campus.
Afro Shade: Afro Shade is the only Afrobeats club on campus. Afrobeats originated from Ghana, but the style is anchored in West African music, particularly highlife music, American jazz, and funk are also added to the mix. This creates a hybrid sound from across continents. We welcome all experience levels and cultures to immerse themselves in the richness of African dance!
Afro-Latinx Exonian Society (ALES): ALES is a club dedicated to discussions about Black and Latinx history, issues, and culture. Everyone (regardless of racial identity) is welcome to join us for good fun and great conversations! In addition to discussions, you can also expect to learn more about advocacy and how to transform your passion for these issues into action. We are also proud to be a space for Black and Latinx students to heal, find support, and connect with one another.
Anime Exeter: Anime Club meets weekly. Come in to watch new genres and diversify your palette, as well as to join a welcoming and close-knit community. We will host casual events such as anime themed cookie decorating, sticker making, Guess the Anime Song, tournaments, and anime watch parties! However, anime is far from perfect. As a club, we recognize that there are many underlying messages and depictions of characters in Japanese animation that are inherently discriminatory. We think it is extremely important to address these issues, and will host discussions throughout the year where people can express their feelings and engage in constructively criticizing said shows.
Arabic Club: The goal of Arabic Club is to connect PEA students with the language and cultures of the Arabic speaking world.
Archaeology Club: If you like spending time with your friends and the coolest advisor while learning about the rich background between the cultural objects you see around us, then Archaeology Club is the place for you! Each meeting, we will discuss the process of archaeology and eventually get some hands-on experience in this field. The Archaeology Club is a club for all. You don't have to be a world-class archaeologist (even though our advisor is) or Indiana Jones to come to our meetings. Everyone of all interest levels and experience is welcome to join us! Put on your gloves and boots and let's find some fragments of history! Do you dig it?
Architecture Club: We're the coheads of the Architecture Club! As one of the oldest preparatory schools in America, our school has amassed a great number of resources as well as an extensive history. During our club meetings, we will learn about the architecture occupation, get hands-on experience with practicing architects, and provide input about what we'd like to see in future buildings.
Art Club: Welcome to Exeter's Visual Art Club! Here is a community for art enthusiasts (anyone pumped about creating) to gather and continue their art learning journey. We aim to provide every resource to pursue or begin visual arts including the space, time, materials, and art theory education.
Association of Low Income Exonians (ALIE: ALIE, the Association of Low-Income Exonians, is a club dedicated to bettering the lives of Low-Income students at Exeter. We host our meetings to allow these students to share their experiences and learn how best to navigate life at a wealthy place like Exeter. This includes everything from social life to assistance programs to making connections with the faculty on campus that can help make the Exeter experience easier for Low-Income students. (This is an affinity group)
Astronomy Club: Astronomy Club is an academic, science-focused group that promotes the study and interaction with all things related to the science of astronomy, including observing, instrumentation, astrophysics, and cosmology concepts.
Athletes for Racial Justice (ARJ): ARJ (Athletes for Racial Justice) invites all athletes of color to an affinity space where we will hold discussions and fun activities around combatting racial injustice in sports. We aim to create an open space for conversation while building a community for athletes on campus.
Aviation Club: The Aviation Club aims to educate and inspire Exonians on the topic of Aviation and the beauty of flight. In the aviation club, members delve into the fascinating physics behind flight, embracing the rigorous study of aerodynamics. Members will have the opportunity to build Exeter's first wind tunnel, fold a record-breaking paper plane, conduct experiments with the wind tunnel, and publish weekly readable articles on Medium, an accessible and well-known platform. Members will even explore advanced technologies such as stealth, by building a radar and conducting an experiment to determine the RCS (radar cross section) of various geometric shapes. Lastly, some community outreach events will also be organized - such as paper airplane competitions (or a step up: cardboard airplane competitions).
Badminton Club: The Exeter Badminton Club! We are a group of athletes that love to play the game of badminton and would like to spread our knowledge about it! Come to learn from a top US player or have fun with your friends! We are low commitment, no experience necessary, and you may get addicted! We also play tournaments around New England (last year we went to Westborough, Mass) and competed against other public/private high schools, if you are interested too!
BASHA: BASHA is the Bangladeshi students club that seeks to cultivate and nourish an affinity space with social and educational activities. Past events include presenting about Bangladeshi identity at the Asian American Footsteps Conference, Bangla cooking lessons and gatherings over meals, and a Karam board game night.
Beat of Asia: Beat of Asia - We are a dance group dedicated to learning/performing choreography by East Asian dancers and/or to music by East Asian artists. We welcome dancers of all levels and from all backgrounds!
Beekeeping Club: Beekeeping Club! Typically, we'll meet on Wednesdays, with possible field trips to the hive or gardens on the weekends during the fall and spring. Come to learn about bees, their role in the environment, and to partake in honey!
Big Red Zone: Big Red Zone (BRZ) is an Instagram account and club that works exclusively with the athletics program to grow spirit around our sports games! We spread awareness of games and occasionally will theme a game. Join our email list for updates about games, themes, and more!!
Biology Club: Biology Club is a community of students interested in the scientific study of life. Students debate in bioethics, learn about the newest biological developments and discuss its implications in society, and of course, engage in thought-provoking labs outside of the classroom. We host our amazing biology faculty and other researchers as speakers to learn more than what just textbooks provide. We also host the USA Bio Olympiad (USABO) competition!
Birdwatching Club: In Birdwatching Club, we will take walks in the surrounding woods and schedule Zoom meetings with ornithologists. Bird watching club is an excellent place to get out in nature and learn more about the birds native to Exeter.
Bridge and Card Games Club: The Bridge and Card Games club aims to revive the declining popularity of the game of Construct Bridge while also exploring other traditional card games. During the weekly meetings, students will learn the fundamentals of bridge, play practice hands, and perhaps compete within the club. Meetings may also include rotating sessions with other card games to keep things casual, welcoming, and varied.
Buddhist Meditation: Buddhist Meditation club meets every week at the Church, giving a chance for students to go through a guided meditation and unwind to bring the week to a close. Buddhist Meditation is a great way to become more aware of the world around us and is welcome to people of all religions. After meditations, we gather together to have tea and talk about how the meditation went, life, and whatever may be happening in the world.
Catholic Exonians: Catholic Exonians is a group of students interested in learning about Catholicism and growing in their faith. We meet weekly over breakfast to discuss the faith, Church history, theology, and Scripture. When we are able to, we will also attend Mass together. The club can also help students seeking confirmation at Exeter. All are welcome!
Chemistry Club: Chemistry Club explores how microscopic processes shape the world around us. Through labs and discussions, we look at the chemistry behind industrial processes, natural phenomena, and various properties of materials. Whether you're a pyrotechnic or not, we look forward to having you join us.
Chess Club: Chess club meets weekly. Anyone (regardless of experience) is free to drop in and play. We also attend multiple tournaments including Nationals and occasionally have special events (like a GM simul).
Chinese Language Society: Chinese is known on the US department of state language scale as the hardest language to learn. Often times, just language instruction is not enough to reach fluency. For dedicated and passionate Mandarin Chinese Language learners, the Chinese Language Society will supplement in-class instruction with fun and engaging competitions through written and spoken Mandarin. The Chinese Language Society will allow Chinese students and speakers alike to practice their Mandarin Chinese outside of a classroom setting and work on their public speaking skills. In addition to speaking competitions, club members are encouraged to participate in essay writing competitions.
Chinese Student Organization (CSO): CSO! Chinese Student Organization is a place for Chinese students to come together and build community. We celebrate holidays together, host activities like hot pot dinners and field trips to Andover, and have fun.
Clan na Gael: Clan na Gael is a space for Irish Culture on campus. Thirty million Americans can trace their ancestry back to Ireland, and Clan na Gael serves as a space for cooking Irish food, tea, music, dancing, and language. We also have larger celebrations for days like St. Patrick's Day, and other major Irish Holidays. This club provides a taste of home for Irish students at Exeter, but also is open to anyone interested in Irish culture at all.
Cricket Club: Dive into the world of cricket with us – no experience or skills needed, just bring a positive attitude and maybe a friend or two. We'll teach you the ropes, watch epic cricket moments, and even play some friendly matches. It's all about having a blast and making new friends, so swing by and let's hit it a six!
CubingEXETER: CubingEXETER is a club for speedcubing enthusiasts to improve, learn, and grow in a community of fellow speedcubers. Activities include, but are not limited to: beginner tutorials for new cubers; individual and relay races for progressing cubers; discussion of new techniques, tricks, and puzzles for more advanced cubers; and eventually hosting the Phillips Exeter Cube Day.
Culinary Club - Cooking and Baking: If you're looking for a break from d-hall or a place to polish up your culinary and baking skills, come to Culinary Club! This includes cooking and baking.
Daniel Webster Debate Society: The Daniel Webster Debate Society (DWDS) is the oldest secondary school debate team in the United States, dating back to 1807. We focus on parliamentary debate, but debaters of all experience levels and backgrounds are welcome. A parliamentary debate round consists of two teams of two debaters attempting to convince a judge that their arguments are stronger than their opponents. Each person gives 1 to 2 speeches ranging from 3 to 8 minutes. Parliamentary debate focuses on logic, creativity, persuasive speaking style, and knowledge of current events. Outside of improving your argumentative skills, participating in DWDS will allow you to join a community of passionate, intellectually curious debaters. We are one of the largest and most prominent clubs on campus, and we welcome all students, regardless of skill level.
Democracy of Sound (Exeter): Democracy of Sound (Exeter), or DOS(e), uses sound, art, design, and poetry to create installations and experiences that activate spaces on campus. All ideas are welcome!
Asian Voices: Asian Voices is a community for all Asian-identifying students! This term, we'll be alternating between discussion meetings and some casual, fun opportunities to get to know each other.
Design Lab: The Design Lab, your PEA makerspace, is open officially for the year! This is a place where anyone in the PEA community can access tools for 3D printing, laser cutting, sewing, woodworking and much more. Open hours for fall these are… Sundays 1-5pm Mondays 2:30-4:30pm Wednesdays 1-5pm Fridays 6-8pm. Clubs, classes and individuals are all welcome to drop in. Feel free to contact Mr. Campbell (sfcampbell@exeter.edu) with any questions or if you hope to visit outside of these days/hours. We hope to see you there!
Digital Music Making (Formerly Electroni: Digital Music Making is Exeter's extracurricular hub for electronic music production and music technology. The club is open to members of all experience levels. Come talk about your favorite artists, learn about digital audio workstations, collaborate with and remix each other's songs or show off your own work!
Diversify Our Narrative @ PEA: Diversify Our Narrative @ PEA (DON) works to diversify the narratives and perspectives taught in our curriculum.
DRAMAT: We are the student-run theatre club on campus, with the goal to make theatre accessible to every student! Join DRAMAT to act, direct, or support your friends from the audience. Joining the email list will keep you updated with important information such as audition slots, director applications, and show times!
Dungeons and Dragons Club: Exeter D&D lets students experience the ultimate tabletop role playing game: Dungeons and Dragons. Join us to fight monsters and make friends on epic adventures in our custom-created world—no experience required. Whether you've never played D&D before but always wanted to try it, or you're a veteran who owns all the sourcebooks, this is the club for you!
Echappe: Échappé is the school's ballet club!! We offer an opportunity for students to take weekly ballet classes, learn variations, and perform classical choreography!
Engineering Club: Engineering Club is a great environment for curious newcomers and seasoned engineers alike! Come use the state-of-the-art Design Lab to design, prototype, and fabricate hands-on, student led projects. We brainstorm, design, prototype and fabricate at least one project every year. Completing whatever thing we build is only one part of the process, however. Engineering Club is a learning oriented club, and we aim to pick projects that introduce us to new concepts as we work. Club members specializing in different types of engineering, design, etc. teach and learn from their peers in an informal, hands-on environment. Engineering club is great for Exonians who are looking to use the Design Lab but hesitant to commit to some of the more time consuming and competitive clubs that share the space. No previous experience is required. There are plenty of ways to get involved if our meeting time doesn't work for you.
Entrepreneurship Club: Launch your own startup! Become an entrepreneur! We will help you create your own business, with the chance to pitch at competitive startup contests like the Diamond Challenge and the Conrad Spirit of Innovation. With training and resources about marketing, finance, pricing, and more, we will teach you to be an entrepreneur! (No experience is required.)
Environmental Action Committee: The Environmental Action Committee is a club that works on projects to make our campus and wider community more aware of climate solutions and tries to keep Exeter sustainably minded. In the past we have brought terracycling to campus and worked with the PEA bookstore to promote sustainable products. Although we will be meeting virtually this term, we are still hoping to continue our work to the best of our ability. This fall we are planning to promote reusable sporks on campus to limit plastic waste from to-go meals and increase our online presence by making a website to help educate our classmates about climate issues.
Environmental Proctors: E-Proctors are advocates for sustainable living on campus. Other than inspiring their dormmates to recycle and conserve resources, E-Proctors implement and are responsible for environmental projects in the dorms, like composting. The ideal E-Proctors is proactive and committed to making Exeter a more sustainable community.
Ethics Forum: Ethics Forum is a club dedicated to exploring all sorts of modern ethical issues in Harkness-style conversations. We will use our time to dive right into case studies about ethical consumerism, research, government, and just about every other area you can think of.
Exeter Association of Rock (EAR): EAR is Exeter's coolest rock/pop performance club! Form your own band and rehearse for our concerts (in the past these have been coffeehouses in grill, Assemblies, after E/a pep rally, etc!) There are no weekly meetings (everything from making bands to rehearsing is student-directed!) so it is really important that you sign up for our group to stay updated on performance opportunities.
Exeter Board Game Alliance: Board Game Alliance is an awesome club where we play board games like Carcassonne, Monopoly, beta testing games, Game of Thrones the Board Game, and so much more. Come to have fun and play games for an hour.
Exeter Business Club: Exeter Business Club is a career-technical student organization dedicated to providing our members the opportunity to learn, explore, and experience business and leadership through our various competitions, projects, and networking opportunities. Not only do we hold weekly meetings where we discuss business principles, invite speakers, and prepare for the FBLA State and National competitions, we also organize interscholastic business conferences, attend collegiate conferences, and visit numerous startups and companies in the Boston area!
Exeter Climate Lobby: Exeter Climate Lobby is a group of Exonians who advocate for strong, equitable climate policy. We attend climate strikes, write to our elected officials, and meet with legislators. If you're concerned about climate change and want to take real ACTION, this is the club for you!
Exeter Computing Club: Exeter Computing Club (ECC) is Exeter's premier computer science club. We build innovative projects that solve real-world problems and learn about state-of-the-art techniques in fields ranging from machine learning to algorithms. In the past, we've built the Exeter Course Map (https://exetercoursemap.vercel.app/), and every spring we run an internationally-attended hackathon (https://hackexeter.com/).
Exeter Economics Association: The mission of the Exeter Economics Association (EEA) is to create learning opportunities in applied economics. Far beyond just money or markets, economics is the science of making decisions, and its insights apply across every field and context. During our Monday meetings, we learn the principles of economics through our robust educational curriculum and frequent engagement with high-profile guest speakers. On Sundays, we bring the focus to current events and the world around us, hosting Harkness-style discussions over dinner. EEA participates actively in economics competitions and is consistently ranked among the top one or two clubs nationwide.
Exeter Film Club: We screen great movies on a big screen in the Forum with free snacks. We bring arthouse, independent, classic cult films, and more to you. Join us for a fun night, bring a friend or come alone to enjoy the power of cinema.
Exeter Fishing Club: This club will focus on getting people out in nature while fishing in the local bodies of water at Exeter. There is a lot of patience required for fishing, which with the help of being out in the environment, can prove to be quite a reflective and therapeutic experience. This club will focus on strengthening the connection Exeter students have for the natural world, each other, and to themselves. Fishing is a great, healthy, and much needed break from Exeter's fast pace and busy lifestyle.
Exeter Improv: Welcome to Exeter Improv! The only Improv club on campus! We are a relaxed, student-run club where members play a variety of improv-based games and create scenes. There is no experience required to join this club and it is open to all! Join our email list to stay updated. We hope to see you soon!
Exeter Investment Society: Learn to invest like a pro! Our goal is to make financial education more accessible. We teach fundamental investment concepts such as different types of assets and how to analyze companies, and build off of them throughout the year in a collaborative, large group setting. Throughout the year, we set up multiple (fake money) investment simulators for a fun way to compete amongst the club and participate in REAL TIME TRADING to improve skills! We also complete in the globally renowned Wharton Global High School Investment Challenge. Along with all of this, we will be providing members with multiple opportunities to hear from executives in the field of finance/investment.
Exeter Jazz and Blues Collective: Welcome to the Exeter Jazz & Blues Collective! Join us Friday nights in the Round Room for weekly jam sessions, where musicians and/or singers of many styles can have the opportunity to come together and play some tunes together. Every once in a while, we will have open mic-style show (with snacks) so members can show off what we've been playing to a low-key audience. Bring a friend, de-stress, and get lost in the music!
Exeter Jewelry Making Association: Exeter Jewelry Making Association is a fun and relaxing environment where we learn different techniques to create unique and beautiful jewelry! Some techniques we might try throughout the year include wire wrapping, making paper beads, soft solder/Tiffany technique, and anything else you might suggest.
Exeter Jewish Community: Exeter Jewish Community gathers together weekly to light Shabbat candles, bless the wine and challah, sing a few Jewish songs, and then enjoy Shabbat dinner together. The EJC is a diverse, welcoming, "come as you are" kind of group. And Shabbat Dinner is a relaxed, fun, and enjoyable way to end a hectic week at PEA. Everyone is welcome – you can bring a friend or two with you if you like – or come to meet new friends.
Exeter Nails: The purpose of our club is to provide a creative and supportive environment for students to learn and practice the art of nail painting in various mediums. Members will gain hands-on experience with gel, regular polish, acrylic, and dip techniques, mastering different styles and designs. The club will also focus on educating members about hand health and nail care, ensuring they understand how to keep their nails strong and unstained. Activities will include practicing on their own hands, collaborating with other members, and using practice nails to refine their skills.
Exeter Pinoy Society: The Pinoy Society is a club for Filipino and non-Filipino students interested in creating a close community while indulging in Filipino culture, food, movies, and more. The club also takes part in events hosted by the Office of Multicultural Affairs and the Asian Advisory Board.
Exeter Political Union: The Exeter Political Union, in its new incarnation, would involve itself with many of the things the old organization did. We will host mock elections and primaries for students, spread awareness for events in the world (from an unbiased perspective) and we will invite guest speakers. Speakers could include candidates for elections or elected officials (such as the governors who came to speak at the beginning of the year), professors or political academics, or other individuals. We will, as Co-Heads also try to stay true to the origins of the EPU and will speak to Mr. Jordan and read Exonian Articles about anything else the club used to do. The club would not engage in any significant travel, creation of publications, interscholastic competitions, and members would not have extensive time commitments beyond about an hour per week.
Exeter Private Equity and Venture Capita: Join the Exeter Private Equity & Venture Capital Club to learn about the growing field of alternative investments. Venture capital represents financing given to start-up companies and small businesses that have the potential to grow, whereas private equity financing represents ownership or an interest in a company that is not publicly listed or traded. We'll discuss what PEVC is, the similarities and differences between them, learn important terminology, practice applying concepts to case studies, and invite guest speakers to share their experiences in this field. This is one of the fastest growing clubs on college campuses today, so come join us.
Exeter Racing Club: Join us in building a racing kart and competing with it! Test your racing skills on our high-end racing simulator, and enjoy watching the weekly Formula 1 races. Help us modify a Ford Mustang, internally and on the exterior!
Exeter Research Club: Want to get into research but don't know how? Join Exeter Research Club! We're going to be introducing the scientific method that researchers use to develop research projects. Well also being doing research projects ourselves along with facilitating individual research projects so make sure to join if you're interested in STEM research at all. No experience required at all.
Exeter Rocket Team: Exeter Rocket Team is where curiosity takes flight and ideas become real rockets, satellites, and stories that inspire. Here, you won't just learn about space — you'll shape it: launching rockets to win national competitions, designing Exeter's first satellite to fly in space, and creating media that brings astronauts and scientists directly to our community. Whether your passion is engineering, physics, event-building, media, or simply being part of something bigger than yourself, this is your chance to join a bold, creative team determined to make history — starting right here at Exeter.
Exeter Sewing Society: Join the Exeter Sewing Society - our Sewing Club! All skill levels are welcome- from beginner to advanced sewers! Learn how to sew, work on a new project or share your newest creations! The Exeter Sewing Society is not only a community but also a workshop to learn and teach sewing skills.
Exeter Socialist Union: The mission of Exeter Socialist Union is to involve our community in more public service opportunities across and outside of campus. Not only do we plan to volunteer at the New Hampshire Food Bank like previous years, organize clothes drives to local shelters, and take part in the PEA Community Garden, we also plan to hold discussion and readings of socialist literature on alternating weeks. We aim to inform more of the student body of socialist political theory, the history of socialism, and the current struggles of the working class. ESU is open to Exonians of all backgrounds and political ideologies. We believe in unity between oppressed groups and plan to collaborate with other Exeter clubs to discuss the intersections of gender, class, race, and the environment.
Exeter Sports Analytics Club: In our PEA community, many members are very good at math and data science. Furthermore, "Athletics is a cornerstone of the Exeter experience." The Exeter Sports Analytics Club's (ESAC) vision aims to create a unique opportunity to bring together individuals to perform data analysis in the sports domain. We will apply what we learned in the classroom to the playing field, improving training effectiveness, game strategy, and team management. These activities will help improve the Big Red Community's performance while promoting the real-world application of math, statistics, computer programming, and encouraging the interdisciplinary collaboration of mathletes and athletes. Exeter Sports Analytics Club (ESAC) aims to foster the collaboration of Exeter mathletes and athletes, optimize sports team performance by using data driven insights, create game changing strategies, and cultivate a community of learning and innovation in sports.
Exeter Triathlon Club: The Exeter Triathlon Club will allow Exonians to become well-versed in swimming, cycling, and running. Through these activities, students will improve their athleticism, resilience, and eventually their competitive nature. Ideally, the club will meet on Sundays around 10 am, and we'll focus on a certain skill through activities, either led by Coach Mills or a student who's proficient in the given area. The school's pool, bike room, and treadmills/track will all be extremely helpful tools in training members of the club to be better triathletes. Hopefully, the club will eventually be able to fundraise and host a triathlon on campus where members of the club, and maybe even external triathletes, will get to compete and showcase their training.
Exeteras: Exeteras is a TTBB/lower voice group focused on performing solo centric songs evocative of classic college a cappella, with the decorum to boot. Throughout the year we perform numerous times both with other groups and also on our own (catch us performing in dorms when it's snowing or flash mobbing elm)! We always have a great time and would love to have you, come audition!
Exie Blog: Created in 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic, we knew that hearing from each other's experiences was the best way to prepare and bridge the gap between classes. That's why we created Exie Blog, a "run by students, for students" website dedicated to preparing and sharing information with new students. Join us to give advice to new students and create content (like blogs, videos, social media, etc.) to give back!
Exonian Pipers: This club will be a place where bagpipers, both experienced and new learners, can come together and practice bagpipes as a band, weekly. The goal of this club is to foster a healthy environment to practice and improve, especially since band practices with bagpipes are essential if we ever want to perform together, whether in the Assembly Hall. The band will also give pipers the opportunity to perform at E/A and celebrated holidays (like the Memorial Day assembly).
Exonians Against Sexual Assault (EASA): EASA is a student-led group dedicated to educating students and spreading awareness about issues surrounding sexual assault. In advocating for healthy relationships and habits, we hope to make campus a safe space for everyone. In meetings, we discuss current events from the media, as well as the culture on campus. In addition to discussions, we work with the administration and faculty to better understand our role as peer educators, and together we work to continue conversation within the entire community.
Exonians for Nuclear Disarmament (END): Exonians for Nuclear Disarmament will be a chapter of Students for Nuclear Disarmament (SND). SND is a national nonpartisan organization dedicated to raising awareness among Gen-Z about the threat that nuclear weapons pose to humanity and uniting young people in urging the U.S. government to pursue common-sense nuclear weapons policy. An official affiliate of both the U.S.-based Back from the Brink Campaign as well as the 2017 Nobel Peace Prize-winning International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons, SND has reached students at high schools and colleges and universities in 7 different states and the District of Columbia of April 2024. Educational activities could range from discussion meetings to informational film screenings. Advocacy work could range from presenting resolutions before student council to meeting with government officials (eg. Congressman Chris Pappas) to talk about nuclear weapons policy.
Exonians with Disabilities Association (: Two hundred thirty-seven years. That is how long Exeter students went without having a disabled student group on campus. We were founded five years ago on the principle of making the previously invisible visible. Of making space for disabled students and faculty to find community and thrive. This includes Exonians who are undiagnosed and those with mental illness, learning differences, TBI, chronic illness, and more. We don't need to be silent anymore.
Fans of Football: Totally new to Football? Don't know what's going on during E/a Football games? Want to meet other avid Football fans? Everyone is welcome! Meeting every week, we will talk about games from past weeks and future upcoming ones. As the season goes on we will discuss trade rumours, injuries, playoff odds and more. Some fun things that we will also do, mock drafts, fantasy football, playoff predictions, team rankings, positional rankings, etc. This is a low commitment club without any required meetings.
Feminist Union: Feminist Union discusses topics surrounding feminism and the intersectionality of feminism, providing a safe space for Exonians of all genders to share their thoughts and ideas. We also hold fundraisers throughout the year for organizations upholding women.
Fencing Club: Fencing club provides a space for fencers/aspiring fencers to learn and practice recreational fencing. Meetings will consist of group lessons for beginners, open bouting for those who are more experienced, and possibly private lessons. The club plans on reviving the use of Exeter's existing fencing equipment, which has not been utilized in recent years.
Fight Club: Fight Club is a confidential peer-based grief support group for those who have lost someone special in their lives. We meet weekly to discuss grief, life, and loss, to just hang out, and to share a meal. There is no obligation to attend -- Fight Club is here to be a community of friends and a support structure to lean on.
French Cultural Club: The Student Francophonie Coalition is a group that explores not only the French language, but also the remarkably diverse cultures of many different Francophone countries along with that of Franco-Americans. During our weekly meetings, we will offer events such as watching Francophone films, eating foods from Francophone countries, and just having a great time.
Futbol Watchers (SOCCER): Are you a fan of the Beautiful Game? Do you have a favorite club (preferably Chelsea) and are looking for others who support your club? Are you looking to get into watching soccer? If so, join Fútbol Watchers!! Exeter's discussion club for all things soccer. We'll get together to watch matches, discuss players, and generally have a good time talking about the best sport in the world.
Game Dev Club: Whether you're a programming ace, an art designer, a music composer, or just someone who likes video games, Game Dev Club is the club for you! Come join us as we learn about game development, work on small projects, and develop a full-fledged game throughout the year! We welcome everyone with a passion for game design -- no experience necessary.
Genetics and Biotech Club: Want to get into genetics? Join Genetics Club! We will be running termly experiments, bioethics discussions, and hosting professors and biology faculty to talk about their scientific research. Expect to learn more about this interesting, fast paced field and have fun with friends. No experience required.
Geography Club: The Exeter Geography Club aims to promote a deeper understanding of geography by preparing for national competitions, exploring different topics, and playing games like geoguessr. Check it out if you are interested in the world!
Geometry Club: Join geometry club to marvel at shapes – from triangles to trochoids! Johannes Kepler insists, "Where there is matter, there is geometry." Yet Exeter somehow lacked a geometry club until the winter of 2022. We will ponder on puzzles, exchange proofs, build our own beautiful patterns, and share the delight of geometric discovery with fellow Euclideans. Prerequisite: a bit of curiosity.
German Club: We meet bi-weekly to discuss contemporary issues in German-speaking countries and learn about German language and culture. We will also watch popular German movies and cook delicious German foods. Feel free to stop by if you need help with homework, are studying for a test, or want to practice speaking German!
Girl Talk: Advocacy for Self Care: This club is a safe space where Exonian girls can learn from each other but also from themselves by finding value in self-care. We will focus on girls bettering their own selves through meaningful conversations about life at Exeter but also through different self-care practices such as meditation, nail painting, face masks and more. Join if you just want to relax and have some fun! We want to provide mentorship to young students and create a safe space for girls on campus!
Girls in Sport: A space for female athletes to come together, support one another, fight for gender equality in sports, and encourage female participation in sports.
Girls Who Lift: Aiming to make the gym less intimidating and create a safe space for all women at Exeter, we welcome members of all experience levels with any gym needs (connecting you to gym buddies, teaching form, providing workout plans, and moral support). We reserve the fitness center once a week and lead group lifting sessions. In addition to the regular once-a-week meetings, we'll host other meetings throughout the week, so you'll always have lifting buddies, no matter your schedule!
Ham Radio Club: This club is about developing the interest and knowledge to become an FCC licensed ham radio (amateur radio) operator (technician level). This club is important because these skills are being lost in the general population, yet ham radio is a useful emergency communications network when cell towers are down (earthquake, wildfire...) and operators are needed to relay messages across regions.
Hindu Society: Hindu Society is a space for those interested in learning about and/or practicing Hinduism. We will celebrate religious holidays such as Diwali and Holi, and come together as a community. We would love to see you there!
History Club: History Club meets weekly to explore anything and everything history. We compete in JV/V History Bowl (history-exclusive quiz bowl) and recently placed second at nationals. We also sponsor other competitions, including National History Day (NHD). Join us for a good time!
Hong Kong Society: Hi! We are Hong Kong Society! This club is a great place to meet Exonians from Hong Kong and share HK culture to other interested Exonians. We are open to everyone willing to join! We meet every week. We also plan on watching classic Hong Kong movies, sharing snacks, celebrating cultural holidays, and making MANY Boston trips to eat dim sum and other traditional cuisine. Come join us!
Imani: A co-ed hip-hop dance group where all of the dances are choreographed by our members. Auditions are held every term and there is no experience necessary. We perform at assemblies and pep rallies!
In Essence: Hi Everyone! We are In Essence, one of the many A Cappella groups here on campus. We typically sing pop songs with an easy-to-follow beat, which is perfect for people who are interested in A Capella but maybe haven't tried it before. We plan to perform in a few concerts throughout the year, and at the A Cappella assembly in the spring. Regardless of prior experience, if you are an alto or a soprano we would love to have you audition!
Indigenous Culture Club: Join us in fostering strong relationships and collaboration with indigenous leaders, raising awareness about indigenous injustice, celebrating native cultures, and working to decolonize our community. Let's learn together and improve our community.
International Student Alliance: The International Student Alliance is an affinity group for international students at the Academy. We aim to bring together the different cultures and backgrounds of students to celebrate our diversity. During club meetings, we will discuss how to process and take part in conversations regarding current events going on in America as international students, while being culturally aware and inclusive.
Italian Club: The Italian Club is dedicated to fostering an appreciation and deeper understanding of Italian language, arts, history, and contemporary culture. The club will host a variety of events such as lectures, film screenings, culinary nights, and discussions with experts, including those from the National Italian American Foundation, to enrich members' knowledge of Italian heritage. While the Italian Club promotes Italian culture independently, it also complements the Academy's extensive Italian-focused programs. These include the Italian language course sequence, the term abroad in Italy, and the Exeter Global Initiatives program. By raising awareness and fostering enthusiasm for Italian culture, the club encourages students to explore these academic opportunities at their discretion. This setup allows the club to act as both a celebratory space for Italian culture and a potential gateway for students who choose to engage further with the Academy's Italian-related initiatives.
Japanese American Society (JAS): A cultural club for students of Japanese heritage or students interested in Japanese culture. Join us weekly for dinner, snacks, and good company. Join us for termly events for (significantly) better dinner, better snacks, and same company.
Ke Kontan: Ké Kontan is one of five a cappella clubs on campus. Our name means "happy hearts" in Haitian Creole, and we are a SATB group exploring various genres! Past songs have included Work Song by Hozier and Lovely by Billie Eillish. Come audition at the beginning of the year!
Korean Society: Korean Society is a great place to meet Korean Exonians and Exonians interested in Korean culture; we are open to all! We meet once a week over dinner (super low commitment). Once a term, we go to Sura, a Korean-BBQ restaurant in Boston, and eat A LOT of food.
La Alianza Latina: La Alianza Latina - Latinx affinity space.
Legal Society: Legal Society is a discussion group about the law. In this club, you'll look at a range of concepts, cases, schools of legal thought, as well as some scholarship. We will also hear from some guest speakers.
Liber: Liber is a student-run publication which aims to breathe life into ancient/classical literature, culture, history, languages, and art. The Latin words liber and liber, homographs, mean "book" (noun) and "free" (adjective), respectively. As its name denotes, Liber is a book consisting of the free expressions of our ideas, opinions, and feelings in English, Latin, Ancient Greek, or any other language. Articles cover a wide range of topics: Archaeology, History, Literature, Beyond Classics, and Exonians. Through Liber, we hope to explore classical antiquity and acknowledge its indelible imprint on our world today.
Linguistics Society: Welcome to Linguistics Society, where we delve deep into the mysteries of human communication, studying sound formation, cryptology, ancient language reconstruction, and much much more. Are programming languages real languages? Can animals understand languages? In what ways and how quickly do languages change? How do you even begin to read a language if neither the writing system nor the language itself is familiar to you? All these questions and more will be answered. Join if you're passionate about modern languages, or want to learn how to decipher like Indiana Jones, or want to flex your logic and computational reasoning skills. Each year, we participate in NACLO, a computational linguistics competition that requires no previous knowledge. We also work on our own constructed language or conlang which, after learning the basic building blocks of a language, we create together during our club meetings.
Lionettes Dance Team: Lionettes is a Rockettes/jazz/commercial modern-inspired dance group.
MATTER Magazine: Come join MATTER Magazine, Exeter's sole STEM student publication! We have been dedicated to making science more relevant and accessible to the Exeter community since 2009. Join if you are interested in STEM, writing, layout design or art! You'll learn to do research, write articles, design issues, and create graphics! No experience required. Past topics we've covered include climate change, artificial intelligence and pandemics.
Medical Anthropology Club: In recent years on campus, there has been growing interest in the STEM fields, and within that science category, many students have taken an interest in medical science. The goal for this club is to educate students on medical topics, and help break down a stigma and get awareness for the people in health care that so often go overlooked. This is a low-key club based mostly on dinner discussions and an occasional guest speaker.
Men's Mental Health Club: Men's Mental Health Club will aim to harbor discussions and spaces of awareness around men's mental health. Through conversations, informational experiences, surveys, and personal connections, we will strive to create a community that raises awareness and encourages support for men's mental health.
Middle Eastern and North African Society: Middle Eastern and North African Society is an affinity club for MENA-identifying students. During our weekly club meetings, we will come together to share food, participate in board games like Backgammon or Okey, and promote conversation on a variety of topics relating to the Middle East and North Africa. We hope to see you there!
Military History Club: The purpose of Military History Club is to allow students interested in the past of armed forces throughout the world to have fun learning and discussing the topic. The club will meet weekly to talk about different aspects of military history including specific battles throughout time and general tactics and strategies. We will most likely display a presentation or video overviewing a specific battle, campaign, or war, for the first 45 minutes. We spend the final 15 minutes discussing and sharing our thoughts on what we learned. The content we will focus on is the process in which military campaigns were won or lost, rather than the political, economic, or social effects of the results. The topics discussed will be land, naval, aerial, or mixed battles from any time period. Armies of the Ages will provide an inviting space for students to learn about and discuss the usage and development of armed forces in an engaging manner.
Mock Trial: The Phillips Exeter Academy's Mock Trial Team was founded in 2011. Since then the team has claimed seventeen individual titles, five all-around state titles, and a top-ten spot at the National High School Mock Trial Championship. Every season, the club is composed of roughly 30 members, split into three different teams. In the teams, students work together, playing both lawyer and witness roles. Lawyers work on presenting opening statements and cross examinations during trials whereas witnesses work on developing their character in direct examinations. Throughout the year, all club members collaborate to analyze several different cases for the three tournaments we are typically involved in: the Yale Bulldog tournament, BG, and states. Members become familiar with courtroom presentation, public speaking, and develop strong bonds with others.
Model United Nations: Model United Nations is a simulation of the real-life United Nations designed to teach its members about speaking skills, global policymaking, and effective persuasion. We host PEAMUN, our flagship annual conference hosted on-campus, and also attend several of the most competitive conferences in the country such as Harvard MUN, Dartmouth MUN, Boston University MUN, and Cornell MUN. Our delegates consistently win many awards and recognitions, including several Best and Outstanding Delegates at Harvard Model UN as well as Best Small Delegation at Dartmouth Model UN.
Morgan's Message: Morgan's Message is a program which is based on Morgan Rodgers, who is a former Duke University Lacrosse student-athlete who died tragically in July 2019 after battling mental health struggles. Although Morgan's life was cut way too short, her legacy of unconditional love remains. Morgan's Message was created by her family and former teammates with the mission to eliminate the stigma surrounding mental health, normalize the conversations about mental health, and ensure that mental health is treated equally to physical health for all student-athletes. As ambassadors, we have access to incredibly impactful and educational slides that this organization has created for the idea of creating Morgan's Message Clubs for students around the world. In this club, we will meet weekly to present slides on an abundance of topics and skills that are useful for students and student-athletes to know.
Multiracial Exonian Society (MRES): This club is a space to explore the accomplishments and struggles of multiracial people in the past and present, as well as explore our own identities and experiences as multiracial Exonians. We are not an affinity group, so everyone, of all different racial identities, is welcome to come!
Music Writing Club: The purpose of this club is to gather students who love to write music to express thoughts, feelings and imagination and promote the love for music writing. At PEA, we have talented students with years of music experience. Some of us play instruments in orchestras and ensembles, sing in choir and theater, or produce electronic music. And with the inaugural Pittman Music composition competition at PEA in 2023, we found students with similar interests in writing music. This competition also opened the door for some who had not written music before and encouraged them to express themselves through music. We want to get together to write music and share it with each other. We hope we can improve our writing skills and compose better pieces to participate in the Pittman composition competition in the coming years, and also to spread the love of music-writing to more students.
Muslim Student Association: Hello and welcome to the Muslim Student Association! We are a community for Muslims on campus to connect, worship, and relax in a safe space! Come join us on for Jummah prayer - lunch will be provided! We also hold various community events throughout the year as well as Ramadan and Eid activities. We're very excited to have you join us this year!
Neuroscience Club: Come to the Neuroscience Club for a welcoming environment where we talk about brains, cognitive science, behavior, memory, Neuralinks, and much, much more! We plan to include fun activities such as brain dissections, discussions about current research, brain facts presentations, guest speakers events, field trips to visit labs, and to attend the Brain Bee competition! We have a goal to potentially have research published in a peer-reviewed journal as well! There is no prior neuroscience experience required. Come for a fun time and a great community!
Ocean Awareness and Action Club: Welcome! Here in O2AC, PEA's ocean club, we work to advocate and raise awareness on ocean issues in our community and beyond through games, film showings, petition signings, community engagement projects at the Children's Center, and more. In the past, we have won the regional competition of the National Ocean Sciences Bowl, planned PEA's first Earth Day student arts exhibition, and designed a digital collage on theoceanlink.com. If you want to take action, have fun, and learn something new about world oceans each week, you've come to the right place!
Origami Club: Hi! We are people who enjoy folding origami! In the past, we did projects like figures, tesselations, modular origami, and even more! We even had a field trip and went to MIT's origami convention. This year, we want to continue our origami adventure as well as going into more topics like designing origami and CP (crease patterns)! We welcome folders and enthusiasts from all levels; please join us as we sail our boat into the unknown!
Outing Club: Greetings Adventurers! Do you love hiking, camping, building campfires, and exploring trails and mountains ridges? Do you want to experience going out beyond the Exeter bubble during the school year? If so, the Outing Club provides ample opportunity to do so; a couple of times every term, we travel across New Hampshire, mostly to the White Mountains, and embark on hiking trips with a small group of students. In the past, we have done day trips to Mount Monadnock and Franconia Ridge, camped by Crawford Notch overnight to scale Mount Washington the following day, and stayed in a cabin by Lonesome Lake in the winter to go sledding and snow-shoeing, and to bask in a stove heated hot-tub!
Outkast: OutKast is the all-male step team at PEA. Founded over 20 years ago, OutKast is the first and longest running step team. Step is a form of dance culturally tied to the black community. Brought to the Americas through the slave trade, step has become popularized with black fraternities and sororities across the United States. Step is the mix of both dance and music, where one's body becomes the instrument. OutKast performances are loud, energetic, and the sight for any and all school performing events.
PEADQUACS: PEADs is the oldest A Cappella Group on campus, established in 1952. We are a co-ed group and take both upper and lower voices. Some of our previous repertoire include songs like Electric Love, The Night We Met, Stand By Me, Bridge Over Troubled Water, Can't Help Falling in Love With You, and Counting Stars. We also have an annual tradition of Valentine's Day Serenades, in which students from all over campus can request a serenade for a friend and we go around to all the dorms and sing songs for those who received an order. It's a blast!
PEAN: Welcome to PEAN, Exeter's yearbook committee! PEAN is open to anyone and everyone; experience with InDesign and Photoshop is amazing but not required by any means (we will teach you!). We are a community of artists who think deeply about how meaning emerges from design, and you can get involved in a way that aligns with YOUR interests. If you love photography, you can become a PEAN photographer (we have cameras available to lend!). If you love graphic design and organizing photos, you can become an Ads, Layout, Sports or Senior Pages associate and help design pages in each of these sections. We also need artists to draw sketches to be incorporated into the book, and business managers who deal with logistics. All of your efforts will go toward building a memorable yearbook that will go out to the entire Exeter community and be perused for years and years to come. PEAN represents the themes, people, and events of the year, so we need YOUR voice to make it complete.
peAnimation: This club will explore the beautiful world of hand-drawn animation and provide a space for fellow artists to come together and animate!
Pendulum: Pendulum is Exeter's premiere visual arts and creative writing club! We meet over breakfast to discuss good reads, cool art we've seen, and plan an annual magazine featuring the best of Exeter's creatives. Come to try out art/writing for the first time, get feedback and hear about opportunities from experienced editors, and make new friends!
Philoglottic Society (formerly Language: The idea for this club is to be a space for students who have a passion for learning new languages to fluency. A typical meeting would involve us discussing strategies for learning languages, tips for finding native speakers, and helping students find languages to learn. Additionally, it will be a time for students to discuss our common experiences with learning languages and finding resources for them.
Philosophy Club: Philosophy Club is a casual discussion-oriented club focused on the interpretation and discovery of philosophical topics. Ranging from modern ethical dilemmas to ancient doctrines, we'll unpack and discuss them all. Be prepared to question, to think, to believe, to trust, and to philosophize!
Photography Club: Welcome to Exeter Photography Club! We're really excited to run the club this year! No need to worry if you don't have any gear, you're welcome to use your phone camera, model, or simply tag along.
Physics Club: Come to Physics Club to learn about the nature of our world. We'll have discussions about a variety of topics and prepare for competitions like F=ma and USAYPT with students across the globe.
Pickleball Pros: In this club everything will be centered around pickleball! At the beginning of the year, our meeting would probably be based on teaching the rules and basics of pickle ball. By the end of the year we would plan to have a tournament in which all members and even faculty can play in.
POMS: POMS is Exeter's cheerleading group! We boost school spirit while having fun, performing at pep rallies and Exeter vs Andover football and basketball half-times. We welcome everyone to try out!
Psychology Club: Welcome to Psychology Club! We're a group of students with wide ranging interests in human thought, behavior, and emotion. Join us weekly over a tasty snack for fascinating conversations, games, and informal experiments that explore the intricate science of the human mind.
Real Estate Club: Our club will educate students and facilitate discussion on various aspects of real estate, including the details of real estate financing, mortgage markets, insurance, and more. An example topic our club would discuss would will learn a lot on the 2008 financial crisis and how it related to mortgages, which is a very important topic regarding broader business and how lawmakers and financial sector employees can prevent such catastrophes going forward. Our club will also help students investigate potential career paths and skill building in the real estate space.
Red Runners: Red Runners is a community for Exonians who want to explore running in a supportive and welcoming way. Whether you're brand new to the sport or looking for a lighthearted outlet beyond competition, Red Runners offers opportunities to get moving and build healthy habits. The club hosts fun relay races and casual running events designed to promote cardiovascular fitness, while fostering a stress-free environment where students can connect, laugh, and enjoy exercise together. At its heart, Red Runners is about making running—and wellness overall—accessible, enjoyable, and social for the Exeter community.
Reel Life Productions: Reel Life Productions (RLP) is PEA's filmmaking club. We work on projects ranging from short films to coverage of school wide events. Club members will have access to all the various film equipment and editing software the Academy offers. This year, we are planning to host occasional, socially-distanced movie screenings for the entire community. If you are interested in filming, acting, directing, film editing, or scriptwriting, we encourage you to join us.
Reproductive Rights Club: With recent attacks on women's reproductive rights, we feel it is more pressing than ever to create a space on campus where we can take tangible action, and where can students can talk about their frustrations. We are going to focus on two main aspects within the wide agenda of this club. The first is going to be educational and the other is going to be surrounding activism. We hope to organize fundraising for women's reproductive rights/justice organizations and a long-term goal of ours is to eventually attend women's marches and in-person protests. We are excited to be starting this club! You do not have to identify as female to join us, just bring your passion for reproductive rights and justice!
Republican Club: Republican Club is an open space for respectful political debate on campus. The club is accepting of all viewpoints and welcomes anyone seeking some great debate. Whether you have some fierce opinions, or would simply like to drop by to listen, Republican Club would love to have you!
Robotics Club: The robotics club participates in two competitive events: FTC and MATE-ROV. You will find information about FTC at https://www.firstinspires.org/robotics/ftc. There are four FTC teams: APEX, EDGE, SURFACE, and VERTEX. There is only one team, MUREX, that competes at MATE-ROV - https://materovcompetition.org/. It is an underwater based competition. Both FTC and MATE-ROV have mechanical and programming subgroups. However, MATE-ROV has an electrical group. While there are subgroups, a few members are capable in performing both roles. Mechanical group design and build the robot but in consultation with the programming group. The programming group ensure that the robot can autonomously function with the use of sensors, and the controls are optimally working. The electrical group (only MUREX) design and build circuits, and integrates it with other commercially available equipment. Each robotics team can potentially have a maximum of 15 members. New members are accepted through an application and interview process.
Rock Climbing Club: Want to get better at rock climbing? Never climbed before? Thought the movie Free Solo was cool? This club is for you! We take trips either to the climbing gym MetroRock in Newburyport or outside to boulder in Pawtuckaway. We also have a new climbing wall open in Love Gym for more frequent weekly meetings! All harnesses and shoes are included!
Russian Club: In this club, we will cook Russian food, watch movies and read stories, discuss Russian culture, and practice speaking Russian.
Sans Hommes: Hi everyone!! We are Sans Hommes, one of the few SSAA(soprano alto) groups here on campus. In the past, we have done a huge range of songs from Take Me to Church by Hozier and Pitch Perfect mash ups :) We can't wait to see you at auditions!!
Science Bowl: Science Bowl is a trivia style competition played between five-person teams that quizzes subjects including math, physics, chemistry, biology, and earth and space science. In the past, we've placed top 16 at Nationals and 1st and 2nd place (last year!) at the New England regional competition. If you're interested in learning about science, or talented in any of the featured subjects, we hope you'll join us.
Science Olympiad: Science Olympiad is an American team competition in which students compete in 23 events pertaining to various scientific disciplines, including earth science, biology, chemistry, physics, and engineering. in the past few years, the Exeter Science Olympiad has excelled in invitationals at Harvard, Dartmouth, and Brown, as well as the New Hampshire state competition.
Secular Society: We will discuss religion in media, approaching goodness and philosophy in the absence of god, and other ways religion impacts us. As students and teenagers, having a space to consider different ways to view religion, as religious people or not, is very important.
Self Care Club: In Self Care club, the primary purpose is to allow students to destress from the school week by organizing different activities that involve some aspect of self care. Being that PEA is a stressful environment, we'd like to provide a designated space and time, not only to socialize, but to take a break from their school work to engage in activities that will re-energize rather than drain them. Some examples of activities we would facilitate are baking, guided journaling, going on walks, arts + crafts, yoga sessions, etc. Ultimately, we wish to educate the members of our club on how to build healthy habits that will lead to improved mental well-being and subsequent academic success, all the while providing the space and resources for them to be able to do some of those practices.
Shakti: A Bollywood dance club that invites anyone looking to learn more about Bollywood and dance!
Ski Club: The Ski Club provides the student body a fun opportunity to ski with your friends! We are currently planning multiple trips throughout the year. No prior experience is required, but is preferred.
Sports Statistics Club: In the Sports Statistics club, we will deal with all things related to sports but through a statistical and mathematical lens, rather than as a typical sports fan. We will begin by doing challenges and competitions related to baseball stats since baseball is a highly mathematical sport. However, based on interest we will move onto other sports members may have an interest in. There may be prizes for the winners of certain challenges! This club is open to anyone with an interest in either sports, statistics, or both! Come for math or just to discuss sports with fellow enthusiasts.
Students of Caribbean and African Ancest: Student of Caribbean, African Ancestry or SOCAA for short is a cultural cooking club, that focuses on making dishes from Caribbean and African culture. The club meets weekly. Anybody who is interested in Caribbean or African ancestry is welcome to come.
Subcontinent Society: Subcontinent Society is a cultural club for those from South Asia (India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Nepal, Bangladesh, etc.) or those interested in South Asian culture. Join us as we watch movies, eat samosas, celebrate cultural holidays, and meet new people!
Sunrise Exeter: The Sunrise Movement is a national climate justice organization, building an army of young people to create millions of sustainable jobs and stop climate change. The movement is connected to a web of organizers, activists, and public officials across the country. It strives to build interpersonal, creative, and technical skills through networking, writing to newspaper editors and politicians, and artistic compositions. PEA's chapter will work on phone banking, collaborating with local hubs, and preparing for strikes and lobbying. Political activism through Sunrise will have a particular focus on racial injustice and colonialism linked to climate change.
Surf Club: This is a group for anyone interested in surfing. We go on multiple trips per year to "The Wall" at Hampton Beach. All skill levels are welcomed and beginners are encouraged to participate. If you know, deep down, that you are a surfer at heart, you will want to be a member of this club. Boards, wetsuits, transportation, instructors, lifeguards, and pizza are all provided.
Taiwan Society: Hi!!! Come to Taiwan Society for good food, a fun community, and to learn more about Taiwan. This club is open to anyone who would like to join! We meet once every couple of weeks and will be having club dinners catered from restaurants.
Thai Club: Discover interesting facts about Thailand and play fun games to learn about Thai culture. And of course, THAI FOOD! (and desserts as well!!) We welcome everyone here, even if you don't identify as Thai. Come hangout and enjoy a stress-free time together!
The Asian Magazine: The Asian Magazine is a literary and arts publication dedicated to amplifying the stories and perspectives of AAPI-identifying Exonians. We publish one to two times a year and host writing workshops, discussions, and fun events.
The Exeter Review: The Exeter Review was born out of a feeling that although an intellectual majority may not necessarily be harmful, if many people in a group share common views then nobody is challenging and promoting tough discourse. With Exeter's values of "exacting inquiry and thoughtful discourse" in mind, The Exeter Review acts on the goal of promoting freedom of speech and diversity of thought. We publish student perspectives on the social sciences throughout the term, acting as Exeter's only nonpartisan political group on campus.
The Exonian Business Board: The Business Board is the backbone of The Exonian, our school newspaper. We are comprised of 6 departments, Advertising, Subscriptions, Operations, Marketing, Accounting, and Outreach. As the only club on campus that generates revenue, join us for opportunities to interact with local businesses, get subscriptions from parents and alumni, distribute the paper, and more. Together we learn about running a business and support our hard-working journalists.
The Exonian Web Board: The Exonian Web is the online component of our school newspaper, The Exonian. The Web Board is responsible not only for maintaining our website at theexonian.net, but also for the development of new digital projects such as the creation of a new podcast series! Web Board is a group for hardcore coders, graphic designers, artists, idea-makers, and anyone else who would like to play a role in The Exonian's online presence!
The Exonian: The Exonian is the oldest continuously-running preparatory school newspaper in America, founded in 1878. Operating as the free student press, The Exonian is not subject to prior review or censorship. Staff of The Exonian have the right to report on all topics that may be relevant, including those which may be controversial. The Exonian will not publish content that is defamatory or obscene. The Exonian is also a community, run primarily out of the newsroom in the Elizabeth Phillips Academy Center, which welcomes students from across grades. The paper publishes a weekly 10-16 page paper. The staff of the paper includes 30 editors, between 25-45 writers, associates from the other Business and Web Board, three wonderful advisers, and a significant body of contributors from across the Academy and the broader Exeter community.
Unite Coalition and Publication: We are the UNITE Publication and Coalition for Change. As a publication, we facilitate conversations on identity, race, and equity in our community by sharing the narratives and perspectives of fellow Exonians so that we may better understand each other. As a coalition, we offer an intersectional space for students of different cultures, races, religions, genders, and sexualities to gather in discussion. Our mission is to create intersectional dialogue, build empathy and awareness, spur action, and maintain accountability. We invite you to take part by joining the coalition!
Upcycling Club: Upcycling Club will center around taking pieces of donated items that would have otherwise remained unused or discarded (clothing, furniture, etc.), and transforming them into new, usable items. This will allow for collaboration between the Upcycling Club and others, as the donations could be sourced from the Exeter Exchange, club members, and other members of the Exeter community. Once upcycled, these items will be donated to local thrift stores, charities, and non-profit organizations.
Vietnamese Society: Interested in the DELICIOUS Vietnamese food, VIBRANT culture, and the AMAZING people in Vietnamese Society? Come check us out! We do everything from gathering to cook a Vietnamese feast from scratch(or our best attempt at doing so) to just sitting down with one another and getting to know about one another's backgrounds, cultures, and stories. Regardless of whether you identify as Vietnamese or not, this is a space where you can just meet new people, create new friendships, and learn more about one another, and the Vietnamese culture! Vietnamese Society is open to ALL, so feel free to pop by anytime, we would love to meet you!
WEVision x PEA: WEVision's mission is to empower women through sharing their stories and to advocate for the female narrative through creative writing, journalism, and podcasting. Be on the lookout for our magazine -- new editions are published each term! Outside of the literary realm, we also plan events that feature prominent guest speakers and introduce academic and social conferences for women to our club members. Learn more about the work that we do to empower women in our community and beyond. WEVision welcomes all genders and all backgrounds to engage in discussions and to work hand-in-hand toward our goals. Come learn, grow, and create amazing work with the WEVision community!
Women in STEM: Women in STEM connects female identifying students at Exeter who are passionate about STEM with alumni in STEM fields. Through guest speakers, discussions about gender inequality in STEM, and utilizing mentorship opportunities, we hope to encourage Exeter women to pursue passions and careers in STEM.
Word: Word is Exeter's poetry-writing and slam poetry club. We come together weekly to explore the creative process and the written word. We will also read and discuss poetry as a group, with occasional guest poets.
WPEA: In 1965, WPEA broadcasted its first shows throughout the town of Exeter. Nearly 60 years later, we're still making quality content produced by students, staff, and faculty. WPEA seeks DJs from every quarter and trains them to become proficient broadcasters as they share the music and stories they love with the Exeter community. A top-notch non-commercial educational radio station, WPEA aims to entertain, engage, and inform the Seacoast area and the world with unique student-run programming.
Young Brothers Society: Young Brothers Society (YBS) is an affinity space for Black and/or Latino male-identifying students where we will hold discussions, play games and learn about useful resources on campus. We aim to create an open and safe space for dialogue surrounding issues that affect us.
Young Sisters Society: Young Sisters Society is an affinity space for Black and Latinx women or genderqueer students.
